title: The flex container
description: display: flex flips the layout mode.

questions.prompt: You add `display: flex` to a parent. What happens to its children?
options: They become flex items and lay out in a row by default
questions.explanation: Children of a flex container become flex items, arranged in a row by default.

questions.prompt: The default direction for a flex container is:

questions.explanation: display: flex turns .row into a flex container so gap works.

questions.prompt: By default, flex items in a row:
options: Stretch to fill cross axis, don't wrap
questions.explanation: Default: nowrap, stretch on cross axis.

questions.prompt: Which value on the container enables wrapping?
What logs the display of children?
options: block-like as flex items with a shared row
questions.explanation: Direct children of a flex container become flex items regardless of their default display.

options: Adds margin to the first item
questions.explanation: gap = spacing BETWEEN items — no edge padding.

questions.prompt: Difference between display: flex and display: inline-flex?
options: flex is a block-level container; inline-flex flows with surrounding text
questions.explanation: inline-flex is a flex container that sits in the inline flow like a span.
